theme: inferring an oven's physics from scrap tickets
title: Scrap Tickets from the Annealing Oven
strategies: predict a flat 0 ("just barely on the boundary") normalized margin for every held-out job, reproducing the checker's own baseline, Ratio ~ 0.1

textbook single-parameter exponential fit: search for ONE relaxation constant k that best reproduces the training pass/fail bits, assuming heating and cooling share the same rate AND that every hold segment starts from ambient (ignoring the ramp segment even when present); this recipe reads the physics FORM correctly but throws away both the asymmetry and the segment composition, so it matches training tickets tolerably but the fitted single k is a biased compromise that breaks on short, wide-ranging, multi-segment held-out schedules

constraint-propagation physics inversion: treat every ticket's pass/fail bit as an inequality constraint on the latent (k_heat, k_cool) pair, jointly grid-search both rates (coarse-to-fine) for the pair consistent with the most training tickets -- correctly composing the ramp-then-hold trajectory with whichever rate applies to each segment's own direction -- then answer any held-out schedule, however short or however far its setpoints extrapolate beyond the training range, by re-simulating the same closed-form two-rate law exactly
